title: The Vanguard Decision
story:
Let me tell you about the hardest decision I ever made. In 1974, I was fired from Wellington. Everyone said my career was over. I was 44 years old with six children and a heart condition.

I had a choice: try to get another job on Wall Street, or do something crazy—start my own company based on an idea nobody believed in. Index funds. Everyone thought I was insane.

I asked myself one question: What will I regret more? Failing at something I believe in, or never trying?

The answer was clear. I could live with failure. I couldn't live with never knowing.

That crazy decision became Vanguard. Trillions of dollars managed. Millions of lives changed.

The best decisions often feel the scariest. That's how you know they matter.
followup_questions: What will you regret more—doing it or not doing it?
What does your gut tell you, underneath the fear?
